slides.title: The best cardio feels *almost too easy*.
slides.sub: If every session leaves you gassed, you're training the wrong system. The engine is built slow.

slides.title: Easy pace burns fat
slides.body: At a low heart rate your body runs mostly on *fat for fuel*. Push too hard and it switches to burning sugar instead.

slides.title: You build the engine
slides.body: Easy 'zone 2' work grows the *mitochondria* that power everything. A bigger engine means more stamina at every pace.

slides.title: Pass the talk test
slides.body: Zone 2 is *roughly 60–70% of max heart rate* — a pace where you can hold a full conversation but wouldn't want to sing.

slides.title: Most of your cardio, easy
slides.body: Aim for *two to three easy 30–45 min sessions* a week — a brisk walk, bike or jog. Save all-out efforts for once.

slides.title: It protects your lifting
slides.body: Because it's low-stress, easy cardio *won't wreck your recovery* the way constant hard sessions do. It stacks with strength work.

slides.title: Train easy, *get fitter*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your steps and movement so you can keep most of your cardio in the easy zone — and see it add up.
slides.tag: build the engine, not the burnout

caption:
If every cardio session leaves you destroyed, you're training the wrong system. 🚶

Easy 'zone 2' pace burns fat and builds the mitochondria that power everything. Keep most sessions conversational — you should be able to talk, not sing. 🔖

Vibe Health tracks your movement so you can keep cardio in the easy zone → link in bio.
youtube_title: The Cardio That Actually Works (And Feels Easy)
